option_text: Team up with someone, but still make your own decisions along the way.
I would stay as close to home as possible, I don't want to go far.
Go through your checklist, finish packing, and head out the door.
Look for more information before deciding.
Feel stuck and struggle to decide what to do next.
Use your own private vehicle (if available) to bring more things.
Take public transport but try to carry all the things you need.
Continue alone and rely on your own judgment.
Outside Finland, to another country where I could stay safely.
Find someone nearby and suggest moving together for mutual support.
Take note of how many bags others bring, and might consider to bring an extra one yourself too.
Pause and consider if you might get any risk before deciding.
Say nothing. It is not your place to police other people.
Look for more information from other sources first, but won't do anything until you understand the situation better.
Call the police, then continue on your way.
Rely on my own judgment and past experience.
Look around and discuss with other people on what to do.
To an official shelter or evacuation center in the area.
Speak up and point to the sign. The rule is there for everyone.
Quite confident. I have knowledge in this and trust myself in such situations.
Take the faster route. Official warnings are not always up to date, and you trust your own judgment.
Stay in your place and wait for your turn.
Decline the offer because you feel uncomfortable causing any inconvenience to their family.
Plan to go, but you need more time to sort things at home first.
I'm not sure. I don't have a clear plan or destination in mind.
Accept the offer and ride with them to get closer to your destination.
Grab your bag and leave right away, you can figure out what's missing later.
Focus on your own move.
Wait nearby and hope the destination will accept you soon.
Try to get more information about the situation before deciding whether to go.
Drop everything and go to the assembly point right now. Time is gold.
Find yourself not moving yet. There is a lot going through your head.
Stop and try to help them.
Encourage someone else who may need it more to take the spot.
Wait and watch where the crowd goes, then follow.
Somewhat confident. I try to make right decisions even when I am not fully certain.
Rely on official authorities (government alerts, emergency messages, official statements).
Quickly choose another destination and leave immediately.
Not confident. I would need more knowledge or training to handle it well.
Feel overwhelmed and unable to decide what to do next.
Wait for the next official update. Until then, carry on with your normal day.
Uncertain. Situations like this are complex and it is hard to know the right decision.
Stay quiet, but if the bus gets full and people get left behind, you feel it was their fault.
Start packing and preparing to leave, even though no evacuation has been ordered yet.
Quickly find another option, such as bus, train, bike, or shared ride.
Take public transport with essentials only.
Stay where you are and wait for someone to come and guide you.
Step forward and take the spot immediately to leave sooner.
Wait and hope the situation improves, so you can move again.
Take the faster route. You're aware of the warning but willing to take the chance and stay alert.
Stay home. You don't trust that leaving is the right move yet.
Stay on the official route. You trust the warning and don't want to risk it, even if it takes longer.
Take small steps just in case (e.g., charge your phone, find important documents) but don't pack or plan to leave yet.
To a summer cottage, or a friend's or family's house in another location.
Sit down and check your phone first. It's better to know more before moving.
Feel hesitant but keep the offer in mind while exploring other options first.
Decline the offer because you prefer to arrange your own travel.
Go, but take your own car instead of the bus. You feel more in control that way.
Check news media (TV news, news websites, live coverage).
Consider taking the spot, but hesitate and look to others' reactions first.
Look for alternative options (e.g., carpooling, ride-sharing) to balance both.
Stay on the official route for now, but keep checking for updates. You'll decide later if it's worth switching.
Check social media and online discussions.
Pack carefully and head to the assembly point within the 2 hours. You follow the plan.
Try to get into the destination anyway.
To another municipality nearby: a town or city further from the risk.
Ask friends, family or acquaintances / neighbours.
